user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook, damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. The suit includes claims for loss of goodwill, mental agony, and a permanent injunction against further publication.

slots.value: Permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform
Malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, mental agony

missing_fields: Date of publication of defamatory content
Specific content of the defamatory statements
assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the specified District Court based on Plaintiff's residence or where cause of action arose.
Defamatory content is accessible to the public on Facebook.

130. Under s 8 of the Suits Valuation Act, 1887, the value of the subject matter of the suit is Rs 130. The parties 
arrive at a compromise whereby the first defendant is to pay Rs 6,000 and the second defendant Rs 5,000 to 
the plaintiff. The court may pass a decree in terms of the compromise, although it has jurisdiction limited to suits 
of which the value of the subject matter does not exceed Rs 5,000.14

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE LEARNED DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}, {{STATE_NAME}}

Civil Suit No. {{SUIT_NO}} of {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}},
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Resident of: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},

...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}},
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Resident of: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},

...Defendant

PLAINT UNDER THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. Description of the Parties
   1.1 The Plaintiff is an individual/business person carrying on business under the name and style of {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_NAME}} and is reputed in the market and among customers, suppliers, and other members of the public. The Plaintiff’s business, clientele, and goodwill are built over time through honest efforts.

   1.2 The Defendant is an individual who is active on the social media platform Facebook and is within the territorial and/or pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court. The Defendant is liable for the wrongful acts pleaded herein.

2. Facts of the Case
   2.1 The Plaintiff states that on or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} the Defendant published and caused to be published on Facebook false, baseless, and malicious allegations concerning the Plaintiff and/or the Plaintiff’s business, with the intent to harm the Plaintiff’s reputation, standing, and goodwill.

   2.2 The said publication was made on Facebook (including but not limited to posts/stories/comments/messages as applicable), accessible to third parties including the Plaintiff’s customers, prospective customers, vendors, and members of the general public.

   2.3 The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s statements are defamatory per se, false to the knowledge of the Defendant, and were published with malice, spite, and an ulterior motive to malign the Plaintiff and injure the Plaintiff’s business reputation.

   2.4 The Plaintiff reproduces hereinbelow, verbatim, the defamatory statements as published by the Defendant on Facebook:

       “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”

       “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}”

       “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

       (Collectively, the “Defamatory Publication”).

   2.5 The Plaintiff submits that the Defamatory Publication conveys, and was intended to convey, to ordinary reasonable readers/viewers that the Plaintiff is dishonest/untrustworthy and that the Plaintiff’s business practices are unethical and/or unlawful, thereby lowering the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of right-thinking members of society and the business community.

   2.6 The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s publication was made to and read by third parties. The Plaintiff will rely upon the Facebook post URL(s), screenshots, and metadata to demonstrate publication and reach. The Plaintiff further states that due to the nature of Facebook, the Defamatory Publication was capable of being shared, commented upon, and viewed by numerous persons.

   2.7 The Plaintiff states that upon coming to know of the Defamatory Publication on {{KNOWLEDGE_DATE}}, the Plaintiff suffered severe shock, mental agony, humiliation, and distress. The Plaintiff’s goodwill and business reputation have been gravely harmed.

   2.8 The Plaintiff states that consequent to the Defamatory Publication, the Plaintiff has suffered and continues to suffer:
       (a) loss of goodwill and reputation;
       (b) loss of business opportunities and customer confidence;
       (c) mental agony, humiliation, and distress;
       (d) apprehension of further harm due to continued availability and/or republication on Facebook or other social media.

   2.9 The Plaintiff states that the Defendant has neither withdrawn the Defamatory Publication nor issued any apology/correction, and the Plaintiff apprehends that the Defendant may continue to publish or republish similar defamatory content unless restrained by an order of this Hon’ble Court.

3. Cause of Action
   3.1 The cause of action first arose on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant published the Defamatory Publication on Facebook concerning the Plaintiff.

   3.2 The right to sue accrued on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when the Defendant’s defamatory publication was made and came to be available to third parties, thereby injuring the Plaintiff’s reputation and business goodwill.

   3.3 The cause of action is of a continuing nature as the Defamatory Publication continues to remain accessible/available on Facebook and is capable of being viewed, shared, and republished, thereby continuing to cause injury to the Plaintiff.

4. Limitation
   4.1 The Plaintiff states that the present suit is within limitation. The Plaintiff craves leave to mention and rely upon the applicable provision under the Limitation Act, 1963, including the relevant Schedule Article governing limitation for compensation for libel/defamation, at the time of filing/final hearing.

   4.2 Without prejudice, the Plaintiff states that the suit is filed within the limitation period computed from the date of publication/knowledge and/or from the continuing wrong arising from continued accessibility of the Defamatory Publication.

5. Jurisdiction
   5.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court and/or the Defamatory Publication was accessible and caused injury to reputation and business within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

   5.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the Plaintiff values the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only), being the damages claimed.

6. Valuation and Court Fee
   6.1 The suit is valued for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only).

   6.2 The requisite court fee as per the applicable Court Fees law of the State of {{STATE_NAME}} is payable. As jurisdiction-specific rates are presently not available in the provided context, the Plaintiff undertakes to affix the appropriate court fee at the time of filing as per calculation and verification. Court fee: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}.

7. No Other Proceedings
   7.1 The Plaintiff states that no other suit or proceeding between the parties on the same cause of action for the same reliefs has been filed and is pending before any court of competent jurisdiction, to the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge.

8. Reliefs / Prayer

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE LEARNED DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{DISTRICT_PLACE}}, {{STATE_NAME}}

Civil Suit No. {{SUIT_NO}} of {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Age: {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}},
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Resident of: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}},

...Plaintiff

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Age: {{DEFENDANT_AGE}},
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Resident of: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}},

...Defendant

PLAINT UNDER THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION (LIBEL) AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

1. Description of the Parties
   1.1 The Plaintiff is an individual/business person carrying on business under the name and style of {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_NAME}} and is reputed in the market and among customers, suppliers, and other members of the public. The Plaintiff’s business, clientele, and goodwill are built over time through honest efforts.

   1.2 The Defendant is an individual who is active on the social media platform Facebook and is within the territorial and/or pecuniary jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court. The Defendant is liable for the wrongful acts pleaded herein.

2. Facts of the Case
   2.1 The Plaintiff states that on or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} the Defendant published and caused to be published on Facebook false, baseless, and malicious allegations concerning the Plaintiff and/or the Plaintiff’s business, with the intent to harm the Plaintiff’s reputation, standing, and goodwill.

   2.2 The said publication was made on Facebook (including but not limited to posts/stories/comments/messages as applicable), accessible to third parties including the Plaintiff’s customers, prospective customers, vendors, and members of the general public.

   2.3 The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s statements are defamatory per se, false to the knowledge of the Defendant, and were published with malice, spite, and an ulterior motive to malign the Plaintiff and injure the Plaintiff’s business reputation.

   2.4 The Plaintiff reproduces hereinbelow, verbatim, the defamatory statements as published by the Defendant on Facebook:

       “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”

       “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_2}}”

       “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

       (Collectively, the “Defamatory Publication”).

   2.5 The Plaintiff submits that the Defamatory Publication conveys, and was intended to convey, to ordinary reasonable readers/viewers that the Plaintiff is dishonest/untrustworthy and that the Plaintiff’s business practices are unethical and/or unlawful, thereby lowering the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of right-thinking members of society and the business community.

   2.6 The Plaintiff states that the Defendant’s publication was made to and read by third parties. The Plaintiff will rely upon the Facebook post URL(s), screenshots, and metadata to demonstrate publication and reach. The Plaintiff further states that due to the nature of Facebook, the Defamatory Publication was capable of being shared, commented upon, and viewed by numerous persons.

   2.7 The Plaintiff states that upon coming to know of the Defamatory Publication on {{KNOWLEDGE_DATE}}, the Plaintiff suffered severe shock, mental agony, humiliation, and distress. The Plaintiff’s goodwill and business reputation have been gravely harmed.

   2.8 The Plaintiff states that consequent to the Defamatory Publication, the Plaintiff has suffered and continues to suffer:
       (a) loss of goodwill and reputation;
       (b) loss of business opportunities and customer confidence;
       (c) mental agony, humiliation, and distress;
       (d) apprehension of further harm due to continued availability and/or republication on Facebook or other social media.

   2.9 The Plaintiff states that the Defendant has neither withdrawn the Defamatory Publication nor issued any apology/correction, and the Plaintiff apprehends that the Defendant may continue to publish or republish similar defamatory content unless restrained by an order of this Hon’ble Court.

3. Cause of Action
   3.1 The cause of action first arose on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant published the Defamatory Publication on Facebook concerning the Plaintiff, which publication was accessible/available to third parties and resulted in injury to the Plaintiff’s reputation and business goodwill.

   3.2 The cause of action is of a continuing nature as the Defamatory Publication continues to remain accessible/available on Facebook and is capable of being viewed, shared, and republished, thereby continuing to cause injury to the Plaintiff.

4. Limitation
   4.1 The Plaintiff states that the present suit is within limitation and is filed within the period prescribed under Article 75 of the Limitation Act, 1963 (one year) for compensation for libel, computed from {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}.

   4.2 The Plaintiff further states that the Defamatory Publication continues to remain accessible/available on Facebook, causing continuing injury to the Plaintiff; the Plaintiff craves leave to urge additional grounds in relation to continuing publication/injury as may be permissible in law.

5. Jurisdiction
   5.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court and/or the Defamatory Publication was accessible and caused injury to reputation and business within the jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court.

   5.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the Plaintiff values the suit for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only), being the damages claimed.

6. Valuation and Court Fee
   6.1 The suit is valued for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only).

   6.2 The requisite court fee shall be paid as per the applicable Court Fees law/State amendment in the State of {{STATE_NAME}}, computed on the above suit valuation of Rs. 10,00,000/-. Court fee payable: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be calculated and filled as per the applicable schedule before filing and appropriate stamps affixed).

7. No Other Proceedings
   7.1 The Plaintiff states that no other suit or proceeding between the parties on the same cause of action for the same reliefs has been filed and is pending before any court of competent jurisdiction, to the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge.

8. Reliefs / Prayer
   In view of the above, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:

   (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of damages/compensation of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) on account of defamation, loss of goodwill, and injury to business reputation, along with such further/other reliefs as this Hon’ble Court deems fit and proper;

   (b) Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his agents, associates, servants, representatives, and any person acting on his behalf from publishing, republishing, circulating, sharing, uploading, transmitting, or otherwise communicating any defamatory content against the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform, and from continuing the Defamatory Publication;

   (c) Direct the Defendant to take down/delete/remove the Defamatory Publication from Facebook and any other place where it has been posted or republished, within such time as this Hon’ble Court deems fit;

   (d) Award costs of the suit in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant; and

   (e) Pass such other and further order(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the present case.

9. List of Documents / Enclosures
   The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents (true copies where applicable):

   Document No. 1: Printouts/screenshots of the Facebook post(s)/publication(s) containing the Defamatory Publication.

   Document No. 2: URL link(s) / web address(es) of the Facebook post(s) and details of the Facebook profile/page from which it was published.

   Document No. 3: Proof of Plaintiff’s business identity and goodwill (registration documents, invoices, GST returns, client communications, etc.) (as applicable).

   Document No. 4: Any communications/demands sent by the Plaintiff to the Defendant seeking removal/apology (if any).

   Document No. 5: Any customer/vendor communications evidencing impact on reputation/business (if any).

10. Verification
   I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 9 are true and correct to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{PLACE_OF_VERIFICATION}} on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}}.


__________________________
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
Plaintiff

Through Counsel:
{{COUNSEL_NAME}}
{{COUNSEL_ENROLMENT_NO}}
{{COUNSEL_ADDRESS}}
{{COUNSEL_PHONE_EMAIL}}

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action anchored to placeholders/duplicative accrual dates; accrual must be tied to the publication date (the actionable wrong) and should not be left indeterminate for a time-sensitive limitation analysis
blocking_issues.fix: Set accrual/right-to-sue date to the publication date pleaded ({{PUBLICATION_DATE}}). Remove separate undefined {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} line or make it identical to {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}. If relying on knowledge, use it only as an additional fact, not as accrual.

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation pleading is not ‘correctly’ pleaded: it does not state the applicable limitation period for defamation (compensation for libel) and instead defers to an unspecified schedule article; also mixes ‘knowledge’ and ‘continuing wrong’ without a clear primary computation anchor
blocking_issues.fix: Plead that the suit is governed by Article 75 of the Limitation Act, 1963 (one year for compensation for libel) and assert filing within one year from {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}. Keep a limited continuing-publication/continuing-injury averment without using it to displace the primary one-year-from-publication computation unless jurisdictional law clearly permits.

blocking_issues.issue: Court fee amount stated with a placeholder without a verified rate × value computation; jurisdiction-specific rates are not provided, so filing readiness requires a concrete amount or an explicit placeholder with compliance direction
blocking_issues.fix: Retain suit valuation at Rs. 10,00,000/- but change the court-fee paragraph to (i) state that court fee shall be paid as per the applicable State Court Fees Act on Rs. 10,00,000/- and (ii) leave {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} as a mandatory-to-fill placeholder before filing, removing any suggestion that it can be left uncomputed at filing.

unsupported_statements.statement: "The Plaintiff craves leave to mention and rely upon the applicable provision under the Limitation Act, 1963, including the relevant Schedule Article governing limitation for compensation for libel/defamation"
unsupported_statements.reason: The RAG context does not provide the specific Limitation Act schedule entry, and the draft does not identify the correct article/period; limitation must be pleaded specifically for filing readiness.

unsupported_statements.statement: "Without prejudice, the Plaintiff states that the suit is filed within the limitation period computed from the date of publication/knowledge and/or from the continuing wrong arising from continued accessibility of the Defamatory Publication."
unsupported_statements.reason: No facts/dates are provided to compute limitation from knowledge, and the legal basis for treating ongoing accessibility as extending limitation is not supported in the provided RAG chunks; the pleading is overbroad as written.
